skill.name: d-molecule-ray-tracer
skill.description: Generate photorealistic rendering scripts for PyMOL and UCSF ChimeraX.
skill.summary: Generate photorealistic rendering scripts for PyMOL and UCSF ChimeraX.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 85 static findings evaluated as false positives. The skill generates rendering scripts for PyMOL and ChimeraX molecular visualization software. The env_access findings were misidentified local Python dictionary accesses (self.config, not environment variables). The external_commands findings were bash command examples in markdown documentation. The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance findings were false pattern matches on argparse strings, table content, and evaluation criteria text. The heuristic combination finding is dismissed because all component patterns are false positives. The skill is a legitimate scientific visualization tool with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Misidentified dictionary access as environment variable access
low_findings.description: 22 locations in scripts/main.py where self.config.get() calls were flagged as env_access. These are local Python dictionary reads on a configuration dict, not os.environ or process.env access. No actual environment variable access occurs in the codebase.

low_findings.title: Shell command examples in markdown documentation flagged as code execution
low_findings.description: 40 locations in SKILL.md where bash command examples in markdown code blocks were flagged as external_commands. These are documentation examples showing users how to run the script, not programmatic shell execution. The Python script itself does not use subprocess, os.system, or any shell execution function.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs flagged as network indicators
low_findings.description: URLs in SKILL.md pointing to the GitHub source repository and official PyMOL/ChimeraX websites were flagged as network access. These are static documentation references, not programmatic HTTP requests. The Python script generates rendering scripts and does not make network calls.

low_findings.title: Misidentified weak cryptographic algorithm references
low_findings.description: 6 locations flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm. These are argparse description strings in scripts/main.py, YAML frontmatter in SKILL.md, and markdown table content. No actual cryptographic code exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation text misidentified as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: 8 locations where evaluation criteria, test case descriptions, and output discipline guidance in SKILL.md were flagged as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate documentation sections, not attempts to probe system configuration.

low_findings.title: Path traversal reference in security checklist
low_findings.description: Line in SKILL.md stating '- [x] No unauthorized file system access (../)' was flagged as a path traversal sequence. This is a security checklist item documenting that the skill does not do path traversal, not an actual path traversal attempt.

low_findings.title: Heuristic combination finding dismissed
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged DANGEROUS COMBINATION (Code execution + Network + Credential access) and SUSPICIOUS COMBINATION (Filesystem + Credentials + Network) at multiple:1. All component findings are false positives. The skill has no credential access, no code execution from untrusted input, and no network requests. The skill is a legitimate molecular visualization tool.

content.value_statement: Creating publication-quality molecular images requires expert knowledge of PyMOL and ChimeraX rendering engines. This skill generates professional rendering scripts with cinematic effects, depth of field, and lighting presets so anyone can produce journal-ready molecular visuals.
actual_capabilities: Generate PyMOL rendering scripts with ray tracing, ambient occlusion, and shadows
Generate UCSF ChimeraX rendering scripts with PBR lighting and depth of field
Apply preset configurations for standard, cover, publication, and cinematic renders
Configure molecular representation styles: cartoon, surface, ribbon, and spheres
Control camera, lighting, fog, and depth of field parameters
Support both PDB ID lookup and local PDB file input
limitations: Generates scripts for still images only, not animations or molecular dynamics
Requires separately installed PyMOL 2.5+ or ChimeraX 1.5+ to execute scripts
High-quality renders can take 10-30 minutes per image depending on complexity
Creates single images only; figure panel assembly requires external tools

use_cases.title: Journal Cover Figure Creation
use_cases.description: Generate a cover-quality render of a protein structure with cinematic lighting, depth of field, and ambient occlusion for a journal cover submission.

use_cases.title: Publication Figure Generation
use_cases.description: Create publication-ready molecular images with studio lighting and anti-aliasing for inclusion in research papers and supplementary materials.

use_cases.title: Presentation Visuals for Structural Biology
use_cases.description: Produce high-resolution cinematic molecular renders with custom camera angles and lighting for conference presentations and teaching materials.
use_cases.target_user: Educators and presenters in structural biology

prompt_templates.title: Basic Molecular Render
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a standard PyMOL rendering script for PDB ID 1mbn.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick visualization of a protein structure with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Cover-Quality Render with Effects
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cover-quality PyMOL rendering script for protein 1mbn. Enable depth of field focused on residue A:64 with aperture 2.0, enable ambient occlusion and shadows, use surface style, and output to cover_render.pml.
prompt_templates.scenario: Journal cover submission with advanced visual effects

prompt_templates.title: ChimeraX Publication Figure
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a publication-ready UCSF ChimeraX rendering script for PDB file complex.pdb. Use studio lighting, enable ambient occlusion, set resolution to 2400px, use cartoon style with chain coloring.
prompt_templates.scenario: Manuscript figure with ChimeraX modern rendering

prompt_templates.title: Custom Cinematic 4K Render
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cinematic PyMOL rendering script for PDB ID 1mbn. Set resolution to 3840px, enable ambient occlusion and shadows, set fog density to 0.3, use cinematic three-point lighting, and set depth of field with aperture 3.0 focused on the center.

output_examples.input: Generate a standard PyMOL rendering script for PDB ID 1mbn.
output_examples.output: Generated a PyMOL rendering script that loads the 1mbn structure from the PDB database, applies cartoon representation with chain-based coloring, sets up default lighting, and renders a 2400px image.

output_examples.input: Create a cover-quality render of 1mbn with depth of field and cinematic lighting.
output_examples.output: Generated a cover_render.pml script with cinematic three-point lighting setup, depth of field focused on the structure center with aperture 2.0, ambient occlusion, soft shadows, and 3000px resolution output.

best_practices: Always verify that PyMOL 2.5+ or ChimeraX 1.5+ is installed before attempting to execute generated scripts
Start with the standard preset and preview the output before investing time in high-quality cover or cinematic renders
Save complete scene setups as session files (.pse or .cxs) to maintain consistency when creating multiple related figures
anti_patterns: Do not request animations or molecular dynamics trajectories - this skill only generates still image rendering scripts
Do not use this skill without the required external software installed - the generated scripts require PyMOL or ChimeraX to execute
Do not combine incompatible style and preset combinations (e.g., requesting surface style with ribbon-only effects) without clarifying expected behavior

faq.question: What software do I need to run the generated scripts?
faq.answer: You need PyMOL version 2.5 or higher for PyMOL scripts, or UCSF ChimeraX version 1.5 or higher for ChimeraX scripts. These are separate molecular visualization applications.

faq.question: Can this skill generate animations?
faq.answer: No. This skill only generates scripts for still images. It does not support molecular dynamics trajectories or animation sequences.

faq.question: How long does rendering take?
faq.answer: Standard renders take a few minutes. High-quality cover or cinematic renders with ray tracing, depth of field, and ambient occlusion can take 10 to 30 minutes per image.

faq.question: What rendering presets are available?
faq.answer: Four presets are available: standard (2400px, basic), cover (3000px, full effects), publication (2400px, studio lighting), and cinematic (3840px, all effects enabled).

faq.question: Can I use my own PDB file instead of a PDB ID?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide a local PDB file path instead of a 4-letter PDB ID. The script will load the file directly instead of fetching from the RCSB database.

faq.question: What molecular representation styles are supported?
faq.answer: Four styles are supported: cartoon (default), surface, ribbon, and spheres. Each style has specific quality and detail settings optimized for the target software.
